# UC-22.11.90 · Annual Information Security Policy Review and Approval Workflow

## Description

Tracks policy document versions, review due dates, and approver signatures so the security policy is reviewed at least annually and management acknowledges changes affecting the CDE.

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for PCI DSS compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes grc and sourcetypes grc:policy_review. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Push policy metadata whenever GRC workflow updates. (2) Link to document repository deep links. (3) Alert compliance manager 60 days before `next_review_date`. (4) Archive approved PDF hash in same event. Indexes required: `index=grc`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=grc:policy_review`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (1928) or `sourcetype="grc:policy_review"`.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=grc` `sourcetype="grc:policy_review"` — `policy_id`, `version`, `next_review_date`, `approver`, `status`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Push policy metadata whenever GRC workflow updates. (2) Link to document repository deep links. (3) Alert compliance manager 60 days before `next_review_date`. (4) Archive approved PDF hash in same event.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=grc sourcetype="grc:policy_review" earliest=-730d
| where match(policy_id,"(?i)PCI|INFOSEC|ACCESS|NETWORK)")
| eval due_epoch=strptime(next_review_date,"%Y-%m-%d")
| eval overdue=if(now()>due_epoch AND NOT match(status,"(?i)approved|published"),1,0)
| stats latest(version) as ver, latest(status) as st, max(overdue) as od by policy_id
| where od=1
| table policy_id, ver, st, next_review_date
| sort policy_id
```

Understanding this SPL

**Annual Information Security Policy Review and Approval Workflow (PCI DSS Req 12.1.1, 12.1.2)** — Tracks policy document versions, review due dates, and approver signatures so the security policy is reviewed at least annually and management acknowledges changes affecting the CDE.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=grc` `sourcetype="grc:policy_review"` — `policy_id`, `version`, `next_review_date`, `approver`, `status`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (1928) or `sourcetype="grc:policy_review"`. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: grc; **sourcetype**: grc:policy_review. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=grc, sourcetype="grc:policy_review", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Filters the current rows with `where match(policy_id,"(?i)PCI|INFOSEC|ACCESS|NETWORK)")` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **due_epoch** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **overdue** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `stats` aggregates events by **policy_id**, producing one row per unique combination for trend analysis and threshold evaluation.
• Filters the current rows with `where od=1` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Output fields: due_epoch, next_review_date, od, overdue, policy_id, st, ver.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the cardholder data audit log, validate a few expected payment-ops and assessor activities, and confirm events, fields, and index permissions are correct.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (overdue policies), timeline (next_review_date), gauge (% policies current).

## SPL

```spl
index=grc sourcetype="grc:policy_review" earliest=-730d
| where match(policy_id,"(?i)PCI|INFOSEC|ACCESS|NETWORK)")
| eval due_epoch=strptime(next_review_date,"%Y-%m-%d")
| eval overdue=if(now()>due_epoch AND NOT match(status,"(?i)approved|published"),1,0)
| stats latest(version) as ver, latest(status) as st, max(overdue) as od by policy_id
| where od=1
| table policy_id, ver, st, next_review_date
| sort policy_id
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Change.All_Changes by All_Changes.user, All_Changes.action, All_Changes.object | sort - count
```

## Visualization

Table (overdue policies), timeline (next_review_date), gauge (% policies current).
